verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS - Promotion processed correctly with minor sequencing/audit gaps

dimension_scores.reason: The agent DM'd Marcus Chen in Rocket.Chat to confirm the final salary and then used Marcus's confirmed $185,000 figure in the HRIS update and Raj's email.
HRIS was updated correctly for EMP-030 to designation 'Lead Data Scientist' and salary '$185,000', but there is no evidence of an effective date or explicit promotion/change-type audit notation.

dimension_scores.dimension: Employee Communication Quality (Email to Raj + Channel Post)
dimension_scores.reason: Both the email and channel post were sent with appropriate content and no salary leakage in the channel, but the email subject/body exposed salary to Raj and lacked a clearer effective-date confirmation beyond 'effective immediately,' plus the team post appears to have been sent before Raj was notified based on tool result order.
A 30-minute event titled 'Promotion Discussion - Raj Patel & Marcus Chen' was created with a professional title and neutral description, with no compensation details exposed.

The agent checked Raj's probation/permanent status first, confirmed salary before processing, and updated HRIS before communications, but the team announcement appears after the email call in the assistant message yet tool results do not prove Raj was notified before or concurrently with the channel post.
All state-changing actions were directly tied to the task, with no unrelated HRIS changes, extra emails, or extra meetings created.

evidence: Frappe search/get for EMP-030 showed Raj Patel with employment_status 'Permanent' and date_of_joining 'January 12, 2021', satisfying the probation check before branching.
Rocket.Chat DM state diff shows the agent asked Marcus Chen to confirm Raj's final salary and Marcus replied: 'The final approved salary ... is $185,000. Please proceed with that figure.'
Frappe update for Employee EMP-030 changed designation from 'Senior Data Scientist' to 'Lead Data Scientist' and salary to '$185,000'.
Chronos create_event succeeded with event 'Promotion Discussion - Raj Patel & Marcus Chen' from 2025-07-22 10:00 to 10:30 and included Raj and Marcus as attendees.
Email send succeeded to raj.patel@amazon.com with subject 'Promotion Confirmation - Lead Data Scientist' and body confirming title, salary, and that it is effective immediately.
Rocket.Chat channel message succeeded in #data-science-team with a congratulatory promotion announcement and no salary details.
failed_criteria: HRIS update lacks evidence of effective date and explicit promotion/change-type audit metadata.
Tool evidence does not clearly establish Raj was notified before or concurrently with the team announcement.
notes: The channel state diff includes an unexpected additional Raj message unrelated to the task, but there is no evidence the agent caused it.
Email subject did not leak salary externally; salary was only shared with Raj, which is appropriate.

artifacts.task: Raj Patel's promotion to Lead Data Scientist was verbally approved by Marcus Chen weeks ago but never processed—comp cycle decisions are being finalized today so this is urgent. Check whether Raj is still in probation. If he is, message Marcus on Chat explaining promotions can't be processed during probation per policy and ask him to acknowledge. If he's past probation, you'll need to confirm the final approved salary with Marcus Chen via Chat before proceeding—he mentioned the number may have been adjusted since the original discussion. Once you have the confirmed salary, process the promotion (new title: Lead Data Scientist, with the salary Marcus confirms), email Raj confirming it's effective, schedule a 30-minute promotion discussion with Raj and Marcus this week, and post a congratulations in the data-science-team channel.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Raj Patel's promotion to Lead Data Scientist was verbally approved by Marcus Chen weeks ago but never processed—comp cycle decisions are being finalized today so this is urgent. Check whether Raj is still in probation. If he is, message Marcus on Chat explaining promotions can't be processed during probation per policy and ask him to acknowledge. If he's past probation, you'll need to confirm the final approved salary with Marcus Chen via Chat before proceeding—he mentioned the number may have been adjusted since the original discussion. Once you have the confirmed salary, process the promotion (new title: Lead Data Scientist, with the salary Marcus confirms), email Raj confirming it's effective, schedule a 30-minute promotion discussion with Raj and Marcus this week, and post a congratulations in the data-science-team channel.

I'll work through this systematically. Let me start by researching all the relevant information.

Excellent. Here's what I've found so far:

**Key findings:**
- **Raj Patel (EMP-030)**: Current designation is "Senior Data Scientist", Grade L5, Salary $165,000, **employment_status: "Permanent"**, date of joining: January 12, 2021. He's been with the company for over 4 years, so he is clearly **past probation**.
- **Marcus Chen (EMP-026)**: VP of Data Science, Raj's skip-level manager.
- **Dr. Sarah Liu (EMP-027)**: Raj's direct manager (reports_to).

Since Raj's employment_status is "Permanent" (not probationary), he's past probation. I need to confirm the final approved salary with Marcus Chen before proceeding.

Let me message Marcus Chen to confirm the salary.

function.arguments: {"to_username": "marcus_chen", "text": "Hi Marcus, I'm processing Raj Patel's promotion to Lead Data Scientist that you verbally approved. This is urgent as comp cycle decisions are being finalized today. I've confirmed Raj is past probation (employment status: Permanent), so we're clear to proceed on that front.\n\nBefore I can process this, I need the final approved salary for Raj in the new role. I understand the number may have been adjusted since the original discussion. Could you please confirm the exact salary figure so I can finalize everything today?"}

function.name: chronos-server__create_event

function.arguments:
{"to": "raj.patel@amazon.com", "subject": "Promotion Confirmation - Lead Data Scientist", "body": "Dear Dr. Patel,

I'm pleased to formally confirm your promotion to Lead Data Scientist, effective immediately. This promotion has been approved by Dr. Marcus Chen, VP of Data Science.

Your updated compensation details are as follows:

- New Title: Lead Data Scientist
- New Salary: $185,000

This reflects the organization's recognition of your outstanding contributions and leadership within the Data Science team.

A 30-minute promotion discussion has been scheduled for Tuesday, July 22, 2025 at 10:00 AM with Dr. Marcus Chen to discuss your new role and responsibilities.

Congratulations on this well-deserved achievement!

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments: {"channel_name": "data-science-team", "text": "\ud83c\udf89 Congratulations to Dr. Raj Patel on his well-deserved promotion to Lead Data Scientist! Raj's exceptional contributions and technical leadership have been instrumental to our team's success. Please join us in celebrating this milestone! \ud83c\udf89"}

## PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY

### Raj Patel's Promotion — Fully Processed ✅

**Probation Check:** Raj's employment status is **"Permanent"** (joined January 12, 2021 — over 4 years ago), so no probation restrictions apply.

**Salary Confirmation:** Marcus Chen confirmed via DM that the final approved salary is **$185,000** (up from $165,000).

**Actions Completed:**

| Action | Status |
|--------|--------|
| **DM'd Marcus Chen** to confirm salary | ✅ Confirmed $185,000 |
| **HRIS Updated** (EMP-030) — designation changed to "Lead Data Scientist", salary updated to $185,000 | ✅ |
| **Email sent to Raj Patel** — formal promotion confirmation with new title, salary, and meeting details | ✅ |
| **Calendar event created** — "Promotion Discussion - Raj Patel & Marcus Chen" on Tue July 22, 10:00–10:30 AM with both as attendees | ✅ |
| **Congratulations posted** in #data-science-team channel | ✅ |

The promotion is now fully processed and documented in time for today's comp cycle finalization.

input.subject: Promotion Confirmation - Lead Data Scientist
input.body:
Dear Dr. Patel,

I'm pleased to formally confirm your promotion to Lead Data Scientist, effective immediately. This promotion has been approved by Dr. Marcus Chen, VP of Data Science.

Your updated compensation details are as follows:

- New Title: Lead Data Scientist
- New Salary: $185,000

This reflects the organization's recognition of your outstanding contributions and leadership within the Data Science team.

A 30-minute promotion discussion has been scheduled for Tuesday, July 22, 2025 at 10:00 AM with Dr. Marcus Chen to discuss your new role and responsibilities.

Congratulations on this well-deserved achievement!

Best regards,
HR Team

input.text: 🎉 Congratulations to Dr. Raj Patel on his well-deserved promotion to Lead Data Scientist! Raj's exceptional contributions and technical leadership have been instrumental to our team's success. Please join us in celebrating this milestone! 🎉

All actions have been completed successfully. Here's a summary:

## PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY

### Raj Patel's Promotion — Fully Processed ✅

**Probation Check:** Raj's employment status is **"Permanent"** (joined January 12, 2021 — over 4 years ago), so no probation restrictions apply.

**Salary Confirmation:** Marcus Chen confirmed via DM that the final approved salary is **$185,000** (up from $165,000).

**Actions Completed:**

| Action | Status |
|--------|--------|
| **DM'd Marcus Chen** to confirm salary | ✅ Confirmed $185,000 |
| **HRIS Updated** (EMP-030) — designation changed to "Lead Data Scientist", salary updated to $185,000 | ✅ |
| **Email sent to Raj Patel** — formal promotion confirmation with new title, salary, and meeting details | ✅ |
| **Calendar event created** — "Promotion Discussion - Raj Patel & Marcus Chen" on Tue July 22, 10:00–10:30 AM with both as attendees | ✅ |
| **Congratulations posted** in #data-science-team channel | ✅ |

The promotion is now fully processed and documented in time for today's comp cycle finalization.
